original: Bryan Allen flies a human-powered aircraft, the Gossamer Albatross over English Channel, taking 2 hrs, 49 min
"Raiders of the Lost Ark" (the first Indiana Jones film) directed bySteven Spielberg, starringHarrison Ford, with story byGeorge Lucaspremieres
Republic of Ireland beats England 1-0 at Euro '88 thanks to a headed goal by Ray Houghton in Ireland's first match at a major football tournament
Egypt (500-1 longshot) ties favorite the Netherlands 1-1 in World Cup game
A 1.3 kilogram chondrite type meteorite strikes a house in Ellerslie, New Zealand causing serious damage but no injuries
Browns pitcher Hub Pruett strikes out future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerBabe Ruth3 consecutive times as St. Louis beats New York Yankees, 7 - 1 at Sportsman's Park, St. Louis
Harry Houdinifrees himself from a straight jacket while suspended upside down, 40 feet (12 m) above ground in NYC
Anne Frankreceives her diary as a birthday present in Amsterdam
Stanley Cup Final, TD Garden, Boston, MA: St. Louis Blues beat Boston Bruins, 4-1 for a 4-3 series victory; first title in franchise history

Danish star soccer midfielder Christian Eriksen suffers an on-field cardiac arrest during Euro 2020 game with Finland in Copenhagen; revived with a defibrillator; game controversially restarts with 1-0 Finland win

Richard Sherman, American Grammy and Academy Award-winning composer and lyricist, with his brother Robert (Mary Poppins; Chitty Chtitty Bang Bang; "It's a Small World (After All)"), born in New York City (d. 2024)[1]
Barbara Hillary, American adventurer, 1st black woman to reach the North Pole, born in New York City (d. 2019)
USSR launches Venera 4 for parachute landing on Venus
Patent for the 'Spinning Jenny', the first multiple spinning machine that revolutionizes cotton spinning, is granted to English weaver James Hargreaves
Worcester Ruby Legs pitcher Lee Richmond throws the first perfect game in MLB history in a 1-0 win over the Cleveland Blues at the Agricultural County Fair Grounds in Worcester
Comet C/1983 (Sugano-Saigusa-Fujikawa) approaches 0.0628 AUs of Earth
US PresidentRonald Reaganchallenges Mikhail Gorbachev to "tear down" the Berlin wall
An Australian coroner's report rules that a dingo was responsible for the death of a baby in 1980
The chemical compound NOTT-202, which is capable of selectively absorbing carbon dioxide, is created
New York Yankees future Baseball Hall of Fame first basemanLou Gehrigcollects 14 total bases with 2 triples and 2 home runs in a 15-7 win over Chicago White Sox
"X-Men" 1st film based on comic book series, starringPatrick Stewart,Hugh Jackman, andIan McKellenpremieres on Ellis Island in New York Harbor
The Big Bang theory of the creation of the universe is supported by the announcement of the discovery of new celestial bodies known as blue galaxies
Queen Elizabeth's Birthday Honours List names The Beatles as MBEs (Members of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire); dozens of past winners return medals in protest
USAF Maj Robert M White takes X-15 to 56,270 m
"Toy Story3," directed by Lee Unkrich and starringTom HanksandTim Allen, premieres at the Taormina Film Fest in Italy - first animated film to earn $1 billion
Fernando "Frank" Caldeiro, Argentine-American astronaut, born in Buenos Aires, Argentina
Gwen Torrence, American athlete (Olympic gold 200m 1992, 4x100m relay 1992, 96; World C'ship gold 100m 1995), born in Decatur, Georgia
Morio Shigematsu of Japan runs world record marathon (2:12:00) in the Polytechnic Marathon in London, England
Raid on the Medway: Dutch fleet sails up the Medway destroying British naval ships and capturing the flagship HMS Royal Charles
British explorer Captain George Vancouver lands on the site of what is now Vancouver, British Columbia
Meteorite hits Uden, Netherlands
Possibly the most severe quake in history strikes Assam, India, Shock waves felt over an area size of Europe (low mortality rate given size of earthquake, 1,500 casualties).
TV comedy "Mister Peepers" starring Wally Cox airs for last time on NBC
Zimbabwe discards its own currency, offering an exchange of $1 for 35 quadrillion Zimbabwean dollars
Battle of Okehazama:Oda Nobunagadefeats Imagawa Yoshimoto in Owari Province, Japan
The newly completed Fontana dei Quattro Fiumi (Fountain of the Four Rivers) byGian Lorenzo Berniniis unveiled in Rome
Patrick Kanereceives the Calder Memorial Trophy as the NHL's rookie of the year
Shakespeare's Globe theatre opens in London, England, replica of original Globe theatre (1599-1642) with performance of Henry V, after campaign by Sam Wanamaker
Anni Albers, German-American textile artist and printmaker (1st textile artist to exhibit solo at MOMA), born in Berlin, Germany (d. 1994)
(Armando) "Chick" Corea, American jazz-fusion pianist and composer; 27 time Grammy winner (Return To Forever; Delhpi I; Toy Dance), born in Chelsea, Massachusetts (d. 2021)[1][2]
Jalal-ud-din, Pakistani cricketer (Pakistani paceman, ODI hat-trick v Aust 1982), born in Karachi, Pakistan
Milwaukee Braves spot starting pitcher Jim Wilson throws first no-hitter in history of County Stadium when he blanks Philadelphia Phillies, 2-0
Virginia adopts Declaration of Rights
Shooting begins on Paramount Pictures' "Dr. Cyclops," the first horror film photographed in three-strip Technicolor
British Amateur Women's Golf, Gullane GC: Babe Didrikson becomes first American to win event; beats England's Jacqueline Gordon 5 & 4
Stanley Cup Final, Joe Louis Arena, Detroit, MI: Pittsburgh Penguins edge Detroit Red Wings, 2-1 for a 4-3 series win; Penguins 3rd Championship
German boxerMax Schmelingbeats Jack Sharkey by disqualification in 4 rounds in NYC for vacant NBA, NYSAC, The Ring and lineal heavyweight titles; first time title won on a foul
70th Tony Awards: "Hamilton" (musical) wins 11 awards after record 16 nominations, Leslie Odom Jr.,Cynthia Erivo, Frank Langella,Jessica Langewin acting awards; "The Humans" (play) wins 4
Singapore Summit between North Korean leaderKim Jong-unand US President Donald Trump - first time a North Korean leader and an incumbent US President meet
John Roebling, German-American civil engineer (Brooklyn Bridge), born in Mühlhausen, Germany (d. 1869)[1]
Homer Neal, African-American particle physicist, born in Franklin, Kentucky (d. 2018)
Yuri Baturin, Russian cosmonaut (SK:Soyuz TM-28), born in Moscow, Russia
Brad Delp, American rock singer (Boston - "More Than A Feeling"; RTZ), born in Peabody, Massachusetts (d. 2007)
John Linnell, American musician (They Might Be Giants - Constantinople), born in New York City
Scott Thompson, Canadian comedian (The Kids in the Hall, Larry Sanders Show), born in North Bay, Ontario, Canada
Dedication of the Mat House / public meeting house at the Maya City of Copán under ruler Smoke Monkey
New Amsterdam legally becomes an English colony and is renamed New York after the English Duke of York
1st naval battle of Revolution-Unity (US) captures Margaretta (Br)
Gas mask patented by Lewis Haslett (Louisville, Kentucky)
Abass I of Egypt signs a contract with Robert Louis Stevenson to build a railway in Egypt, first stage opens 1854 between Alexandria and Kafer Eassa - first railway in Africa
